docs.title: Welcome to TheRoundersKnowledge
docs.text: This is the documentation site for TheRoundersKnowledge.

docs.title: Quick Links
docs.text:
• Getting Started - Start here to learn the basics
  • Contribute - How to contribute to this project

docs.title: About This Project
docs.text:
TheRoundersKnowledge is a repository for knowledge, documentation, and resources.

Last updated: May 2026

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text: Thanks for your interest in contributing! Here's how to get started.

docs.title: How to Contribute
docs.text:
1. Create a new branch for your changes: 

    git checkout -b feature/your-feature-name

  2. Add or edit documentation in the docs/ folder using Markdown

  3. Update mkdocs.yml if you're adding a new page: 

    nav:
  - Home: index.md
  - Getting Started: getting-started.md
  - Your New Page: your-page.md

  4. Test locally: 

    mkdocs serve

  5. Commit and push your changes: 

    git add .
git commit -m "Add: description of changes"
git push origin feature/your-feature-name

  6. Create a pull request on GitHub

docs.title: Building for Production
docs.text:
To build the static site for deployment: 

mkdocs build

This generates the site/ folder with all HTML files ready for hosting.

docs.title: Style Guide
docs.text:
• Use clear, concise language
  • Include code examples where helpful
  • Keep pages focused on one topic
  • Use proper Markdown formatting

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text: Welcome! This guide will help you get up and running.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text: Before you begin, make sure you have: - Python 3.7+ installed - Git - A text editor

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
1. Clone the repository: 

    git clone https://github.com/yevhenZ-rounds/TheRoundersKnowledge.git
cd TheRoundersKnowledge

  2. Install MkDocs and dependencies: 

    pip install mkdocs mkdocs-material

  3. Build the documentation locally: 

    mkdocs serve

The site will be available at http://localhost:8000

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Check out the Contribute guide to learn how to add content
  • Explore the documentation structure
  • Start adding your own pages!
